guardrails.title: API route protection inventory must stay current
guardrails.risk: Sensitive/public endpoint changes can drift without governance visibility.
guardrails.notes: Controller inventory and status model are validated against source controllers and explicit allowlist.

guardrails.title: Development/demo endpoints must remain environment-gated
guardrails.risk: Development or demo routes can be exposed in production.
guardrails.notes: Suspicious controller names require IsDevelopment or explicit allowlist reason.

guardrails.title: High-confidence secret logging patterns are blocked
guardrails.risk: Credentials and secret material can leak through logs/source.
guardrails.notes: High-confidence logger/console patterns are blocked; safe policy docs and sanitizers are excluded.

guardrails.title: Authentication defaults preserve compatibility and avoid secret hardcoding
guardrails.risk: Accidental forced-login rollout or leaked static API keys in config.
guardrails.notes: Checks appsettings defaults and authentication boundary documentation consistency.

guardrails.title: Rate-limiting defaults remain compatibility-safe and key-safe
guardrails.risk: Accidental throttling rollout or raw API key use in limiter partitioning.
guardrails.notes: Validates defaults, non-claims, and fingerprint-vs-raw-key behavior.

guardrails.title: Audit metadata sanitization and payload-safe event registry are enforced
guardrails.risk: Audit trail can leak secret or full payload data.
guardrails.notes: Checks sanitizer behavior and registry allowPayload policy.

guardrails.title: InMemory provider production risk must be explicit and controlled
guardrails.risk: Accidental production reliance on non-durable providers.
guardrails.notes: Allows transitional InMemory defaults only when explicitly documented as non-durable in security inventory.

guardrails.title: False security/compliance claims are blocked in docs
guardrails.risk: Overclaiming security/compliance can mislead users and reviewers.
guardrails.notes: Forbidden positive claims are blocked outside non-claim/future/roadmap contexts.

guardrails.title: Frontend source must not hardcode real tokens/API keys
guardrails.risk: Client-side secret material leakage and insecure token handling.
guardrails.notes: High-confidence hardcoded token/key patterns are blocked in frontend source.

guardrails.title: Tenant isolation matrix must stay aligned with protected endpoint rollout
guardrails.risk: Cross-tenant expectations can drift from staged protected endpoint behavior.
guardrails.notes: Validates tenant-isolation-integration-matrix.md/json/schema plus endpoint inventory and test coverage references.

guardrails.title: Persistence-backed ownership migrations must stay append-only and documented
guardrails.risk: Tenant ownership schema changes can become destructive or overclaim isolation guarantees.
guardrails.notes: Validates P5-16A documentation, roadmap inventory, migration safety, and model snapshot fields.

guardrails.title: Tenant-aware query isolation services must remain explicit and documented
guardrails.risk: Query isolation services can drift into hidden global filtering or overclaim full tenant isolation.
guardrails.notes: Validates P5-16B documentation, service contracts, production inventory, and no global EF query filter/full-isolation claims.

guardrails.title: Tenant-aware read controller integration must stay rollout-gated and documented
guardrails.risk: Controller-level tenant-aware read integration can drift from staged rollout constraints or overclaim full isolation.
guardrails.notes: Validates P5-16C documentation, matrix metadata for ProjectsRead/BuildingsRead, and readiness inventory references while keeping workflow integration staged.

guardrails.title: Workflow tenant-aware read integration must stay rollout-gated and documented
guardrails.risk: Workflow read/history tenant-aware integration can drift from staged rollout constraints or overclaim full isolation.
guardrails.notes: Validates P5-16D documentation, workflow matrix metadata, readiness inventory linkage, and DI registration for workflow tenant-aware read service.

guardrails.title: Workflow ownership metadata coverage claims must stay evidence-based and documented
guardrails.risk: Workflow/scenario/job ownership coverage claims can overstate completeness or drift from resolver/test reality.
guardrails.notes: Validates P5-17 metadata coverage docs/json/schema, workflow matrix metadataCoverage fields, and readiness inventory linkage.

guardrails.title: Ownership backfill strategy and evidence model must stay explicit before apply execution
guardrails.risk: Backfill execution readiness can drift without dry-run metrics, safety checks, and governance gate documentation.
guardrails.notes: Validates P6-00 strategy/evidence docs and schemas, strategy-only mode, non-claims, roadmap linkage, and no false backfill-complete claims.

guardrails.title: Ownership backfill dry-run tool must remain no-write and apply-disabled in P6-01
guardrails.risk: Dry-run tooling can accidentally drift into write behavior or overclaim execution readiness.
guardrails.notes: Validates tool project presence, apply rejection, no write-path indicators, artifact ignore rules, and P6-01 roadmap/guardrail linkage.

guardrails.title: Ownership backfill database dry-run scanner must remain read-only in P6-02
guardrails.risk: Database dry-run scanner can drift into write behavior or overclaim backfill execution readiness.
guardrails.notes: Validates scanner file presence, no SaveChanges/destructive SQL patterns in tool source, and P6-02 documentation/roadmap linkage.

guardrails.title: Ownership backfill evidence gates must enforce unresolved-threshold policy in P6-03
guardrails.risk: Dry-run evidence can be accepted without consistent threshold/ambiguity validation, increasing future apply-mode risk.
guardrails.notes: Validates validate-evidence command docs, gate result artifact ignore rules, no-write constraints in tool source, and P6-03 inventory/strategy linkage.

guardrails.title: Ownership backfill apply-mode design must remain disabled and no-write in P6-04
guardrails.risk: Apply-mode scaffolding can drift into accidental write execution or overclaim backfill completion.
guardrails.notes: Validates apply design docs/json/schema, disabled apply CLI behavior, no SaveChanges/destructive SQL in tool source, and P6-04 inventory linkage.

guardrails.title: Ownership backfill plan generator must remain plan-only and no-write in P6-05
guardrails.risk: Plan generation scaffolding can drift into accidental write execution or overclaim apply/backfill completion.
guardrails.notes: Validates plan-apply command presence, apply command disabled status, plan artifact ignore rules, no SaveChanges/destructive SQL in tool source, and P6-05 inventory linkage.

guardrails.title: Ownership backfill plan sign-off gate must remain no-write and hash-bound in P6-06
guardrails.risk: Plan sign-off scaffolding can drift into weak hash validation, accidental write execution, or overclaim apply/backfill completion.
guardrails.notes: Validates signoff-plan command presence, plan hash/signoff governance linkage, signoff artifact ignore rules, no SaveChanges/destructive SQL in tool source, and P6-06 inventory linkage.

guardrails.title: Ownership backfill test-only apply rehearsal must stay no-write and production-disabled in P6-07
guardrails.risk: Test-only rehearsal scaffolding can drift into production write wiring or overclaim backfill execution readiness.
guardrails.notes: Validates test-only executor presence, CLI apply disabled status, rehearsal artifact ignore rules, no SaveChanges/destructive SQL in tool source, and P6-07 inventory linkage.

guardrails.title: Ownership backfill apply-enablement readiness gate must remain no-write and hash-chain consistent in P6-08
guardrails.risk: Readiness validation can drift from artifact-chain consistency and rollback evidence requirements, creating unsafe apply assumptions.
guardrails.notes: Validates validate-apply-readiness command presence, apply-readiness artifact ignore rules, hash-chain/readiness-link documentation, no SaveChanges/destructive SQL in tool source, and P6-08 inventory linkage while apply remains disabled.

guardrails.title: Ownership backfill production apply enablement proposal must remain policy-bound and no-write in P6-09
guardrails.risk: Production apply enablement can bypass evidence, approval, backup, rollback, and change-management policy requirements.
guardrails.notes: Validates proposal/template docs and descriptors, ApplyInputHash policy linkage, guardrail/inventory references, no false apply-enabled claims, and apply-disabled posture in P6-09.

guardrails.title: Ownership backfill staging apply runbook must remain policy-bound and no-write in P6-10
guardrails.risk: Staging apply enablement can bypass evidence, operator, backup, rollback, or promotion controls.
guardrails.notes: Validates staging runbook/checklist docs and descriptors, ApplyInputHash staging requirements, guardrail/inventory references, no false staging/prod apply-enabled claims, and apply-disabled posture in P6-10.

guardrails.title: Ownership backfill staging apply executor design must remain no-write and disabled in P6-11
guardrails.risk: Staging executor enablement can bypass environment, preflight, schema, backup, and rollback protections.
guardrails.notes: Validates staging executor design docs/json/schema, staging preflight command/contract presence, disabled executor behavior, no-write source constraints, and P6-11 roadmap linkage.

guardrails.title: Ownership backfill staging post-run evidence acceptance must remain no-write and disabled in P6-12
guardrails.risk: Staging post-run acceptance can drift from hash-chain, rollback, and evidence completeness requirements.
guardrails.notes: Validates staging post-run evidence docs/json/schema, validate-staging-acceptance command presence, acceptance artifact ignore rules, no-write source constraints, and P6-12 roadmap linkage while apply remains disabled.

guardrails.title: Ownership backfill production promotion readiness must remain no-write and disabled in P6-13
guardrails.risk: Production promotion readiness can drift from cross-environment evidence separation, TTL/re-approval, and change-request binding requirements.
guardrails.notes: Validates production promotion readiness docs/json/schema, validate-production-promotion command presence, decision artifact ignore rules, no-write source constraints, and P6-13 roadmap linkage while apply remains disabled.

guardrails.title: Ownership backfill manual write-path enablement decision must remain manual-only and no-write in P6-14
guardrails.risk: Manual enablement decisions can drift from hash binding, approval, TTL, and no-write boundary requirements.
guardrails.notes: Validates manual write-path decision docs/json/schema/templates, required hash and approval bindings, inventory and guardrail linkage, and no false apply-enabled or backfill-executed claims.

guardrails.title: Ownership backfill apply enablement architecture review must remain no-write and no-wiring in P6-15
guardrails.risk: Future write-path changes can bypass disabled-apply boundary, environment hard-deny, hash-chain, rollback completeness, and no-secrets/no-destructive-sql invariants.
guardrails.notes: Validates P6-15 architecture review docs/json/schema/templates, required invariants/checks, inventory linkage, source-level no-wiring guards, and no false apply-enabled or backfill-executed claims.

guardrails.title: Post-P6 governance audit and release boundary review must remain no-write and claims-consistent in P7-00
guardrails.risk: Post-P6 drift can introduce inconsistent claims, hidden write-path wiring risk, or release-boundary ambiguity.
guardrails.notes: Validates post-P6 audit docs/json/schema, security governance index artifacts, P7-00 roadmap linkage, disabled release-boundary flags, and no false apply-enabled/backfill-executed/full-isolation claims.

guardrails.title: Canonical security release boundary must remain explicit and disabled-safe in P7-01
guardrails.risk: Enabled/disabled capability claims can drift and accidentally imply write-path or security posture changes.
guardrails.notes: Validates security-release-boundary docs/json/schema, disabled flags, forbidden claims list, and non-claim posture.

guardrails.title: Security governance index/status normalization must remain canonical in P7-01
guardrails.risk: Governance document categories, statuses, and canonical roles can drift and create conflicting release-boundary claims.
guardrails.notes: Validates normalized index entries, canonical roles, vocabulary-backed statuses, and canonical release-boundary linkage.

guardrails.title: Governance test consolidation must preserve no-write safety and disabled-boundary guarantees in P7-02
guardrails.risk: Test consolidation can unintentionally remove critical safety assertions or weaken guardrail coverage.
guardrails.notes: Validates consolidation report artifacts, preserved guardrail list, and roadmap/guardrail linkage while runtime and write-path remain unchanged.

guardrails.title: Ownership backfill CLI UX normalization must preserve disabled/no-write boundaries
guardrails.risk: CLI UX changes can drift exit-code semantics, hide disabled-boundary warnings, or leak secret-like values in output.
guardrails.notes: Validated by OwnershipBackfillCliCommandInventoryTests, OwnershipBackfillCliRedactionTests, and OwnershipBackfillCliExitCodeConsistencyTests.

guardrails.title: Release-ready observability diagnostics must remain explicit without weakening gate semantics
guardrails.risk: Release-ready script/tool updates can hide failures, remove timing visibility, or leak sensitive values.
guardrails.notes: Validated by ReleaseReadyScriptObservabilityTests and P7ReleaseReadyObservabilityAuditTests.

guardrails.title: CI/GitHub checks visibility contract must remain explicit and safe
guardrails.risk: CI workflow/check visibility can drift and hide gate status or introduce unsafe apply-path commands.
guardrails.notes: Validated by P7CiGithubChecksVisibilityAuditTests and P7CiWorkflowInventoryTests.

guardrails.title: Route inventory classification and claims consistency automation must remain enforced in P7-06
guardrails.risk: Route inventory, protection stage, tenant-scope, and claim posture can drift from controller routes and rollout docs.
guardrails.notes: Validated by P7RouteInventoryCoverageTests, P7RouteClaimsConsistencyTests, P7RouteOperationalCategoryConsistencyTests, P7RouteTenantScopeConsistencyTests, and P7ProtectionStageConsistencyTests.

guardrails.title: Security docs map and ADR boundary must remain explicit and claims-safe in P7-07
guardrails.risk: Documentation ownership and architecture-boundary decisions can drift and weaken governance clarity for future write-path decisions.
guardrails.notes: Validated by P7SecurityDocsMapTests and P7SecurityAdrTests.

guardrails.title: Security ADR decision matrix and future ADR backlog must remain explicit and boundary-safe in P7-08
guardrails.risk: Architecture decision ownership can drift across docs and weaken disabled-boundary guarantees for future write-path decisions.
guardrails.notes: Validated by P7SecurityArchitectureDecisionMatrixTests and P7FutureSecurityAdrBacklogTests.

guardrails.title: Engineering/domain architecture audit artifacts must remain explicit and no-change scoped in P8-00
guardrails.risk: Architecture hardening decisions can drift into undocumented runtime, API, or calculation-physics changes.
guardrails.notes: Validated by P8EngineeringDomainArchitectureAuditTests, P8AssistantEngineerArchitectureMapTests, P8LegacyAndDeadCodeInventoryTests, and P8ScriptsToolsInventoryTests.

nonClaims: No full multi-tenant isolation claim yet.
No external identity provider integration claim.
No claim that all API endpoints are protected yet.
